reason: Both answers correctly identify alert hygiene as the primary fix, affirm the 6-person weekly rotation is sustainable once noise is reduced, and warn against restructuring the schedule before fixing signal. Both give concrete audit steps and a paging budget. A explicitly proposes a recurring weekly page-review ritual, matching the review-every-page key point; B reads more like a one-time cleanup with a threshold-triggered review, slightly weaker on that point, but adds a useful tactic of routing non-urgent alerts to business hours. Neither mentions reducing sprint commitment during on-call weeks. The two are very close in quality with minor, offsetting differences.
